2603.19307.title: Exploring Subnetwork Interactions in Heterogeneous Brain Network via Prior-Informed Graph Learning
2603.19307.paper_summary: Modeling the complex interactions among functional subnetworks is crucial for the diagnosis of mental disorders and the identification of functional pathways. However, learning the interactions of the underlying subnetworks remains a significant challenge for existing Transformer-based methods due to the limited number of training samples. To address these challenges, we propose KD-Brain, a Prior-Informed Graph Learning framework for explicitly encoding prior knowledge to guide the learning process. Specifically, we design a Semantic-Conditioned Interaction mechanism that injects semantic priors into the attention query, explicitly navigating the subnetwork interactions based on their functional identities. Furthermore, we introduce a Pathology-Consistent Constraint, which regularizes the model optimization by aligning the learned interaction distributions with clinical priors. Additionally, KD-Brain leads to state-of-the-art performance on a wide range of disorder diagnosis tasks and identifies interpretable biomarkers consistent with psychiatric pathophysiology. Our code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/KDBrain.
2603.19307.paper_summary_zh: 建模功能子網絡之間的複雜互動對於精神疾病的診斷和功能通路的識別至關重要。<lb/>然而，由於訓練樣本數量有限，學習潛在子網絡的互動對於現有的基於Transformer的方法仍然是一個重大挑戰。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了KD-Brain，一個優先知識引導的圖學習框架，用於明確編碼先驗知識以指導學習過程。<lb/>具體而言，我們設計了一種語義條件互動機制，將語義先驗注入到注意力查詢中，明確地根據其功能身份導航子網絡互動。<lb/>此外，我們引入了一個病理一致性約束，通過使學習的互動分佈與臨床先驗對齊來正則化模型優化。<lb/>此外，KD-Brain在各種疾病診斷任務中達到了最先進的性能，並識別出與精神病理生理學一致的可解釋生物標記。<lb/>我們的代碼可在 https://anonymous.4open.science/r/KDBrain 獲取。
2603.19307.author: Siyu Liu et.al.
2603.19307.authors: Siyu Liu, Guangqi Wen, Peng Cao, Jinzhu Yang, Xiaoli Liu, Fei Wang, Osmar R. Zaiane
